name: HNMS O 14 (N 14 / P 14)
events: The history of HrMs O 14 as compiled on this page is extracted from the patrol reports and logbooks of this submarine found at the British archives at Kew, London and the Dutch archives at The Hague, Netherlands.
This page was last updated in February 2019.
14 May 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) was docked at Willemstad, Curacoa, Netherlands West Indies. (1)
22 Jun 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) left dock. (1)
2 Sep 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) ended a refit at Curacoa. During the next few dails trials were conducted before she will proceed to the United Kingdom. (1)
9 Sep 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) was docked at Curacoa, Netherlands West Indies to paint the hull under the waterline. (1)
10 Sep 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) left dock. (1)
12 Sep 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) departed Curacoa for Bermuda.
For the daily positions of HrMs O 14 during this passage see the map below.
18 Sep 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) arrived at Bermuda. (1)
21 Sep 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) departed Bermuda for Halifax, Canada. (1)
25 Sep 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) arrived at Halifax. At Halifax some minor repairs were made. (1)
8 Oct 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) departed Halifax for the U.K. She made the passage with convoy HX-79. (1)
20 Oct 1940 At 0123 hours (zone ?) HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) is narrowly missed by two torpedoes during an attack on the convoy by German u-boats.
At 0625 hours the sound of a torpedo passing underneath O 14 is heard. The torpedo wake was not seen from the bridge. (1)
22 Oct 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) arrived at Rothesay. (1)
3 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) shifts from Rothesay to Campbeltown. (1)
4 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (1)
5 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (1)
6 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (1)
7 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (1)
9 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (1)
11 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (1)
12 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (1)
13 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (1)
14 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) shifts from Campbeltown to Rothesay. (1)
20 Nov 1940 HMS H 33 (Lt. R.S. Brookes, DSC, RN) and HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) departed Rothesay for Tobermory. She was escorted by HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (2)
21 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) arrived at Tobermory. From here she assists in A/S exercises. (1)
23 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS La Capricieuse (Lt.Cdr. S.A. Jones, RNR). (1)
24 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Tulip (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.K. Pollock, RN) and HMS Rumba (T/Lt. N.E. Hendy, RNVR). (1)
27 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) shifted from Tobermory to Oban to fuel and then back to Tobermory. (3)
28 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Freesia (Lt.Cdr. T.G. Hill, RNR) and HMS Columbine (Lt. L.A. Sayers, RNR). (3)
29 Nov 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Columbine (Lt. L.A. Sayers, RNR) and HMS Cape Mariato (T/Lt. H.T.S. Clouston, RNVR). (3)
2 Dec 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Columbine (Lt. L.A. Sayers, RNR). (3)
4 Dec 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Hollyhock (Lt. T.E. Davies, OBE, RNR) and HMS Delphinium (Cdr.(Retd.) R.L. Spalding, RN). (3)
7 Dec 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Hollyhock (Lt. T.E. Davies, OBE, RNR) and HMS Delphinium (Cdr.(Retd.) R.L. Spalding, RN). (3)

10 Dec 1940 HrMs O 9 (Lt. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) and HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) proceeded from Tobermory to Oban escorted by HMS Martinetta (T/S.Lt. J.A. Brightman, RNVR) and after embarking fuel, oil and fresh water O 9 and HMS Martinetta returned to Tobermory. (4)
12 Dec 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) proceeded from Oban to Tobermory escorted by HMS Martinetta (T/S.Lt. J.A. Brightman, RNVR). (3)

13 Dec 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Marguerite (Lt.Cdr. A.N. Blundell, RNR), HMS Pelican (Cdr. L.A.K. Boswell, DSO, RN) and the A/P trawler Duchesse de Brabant. (3)
19 Dec 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with the A/P trawler Duchesse de Brabant and HMS Lady Rosemary (T/Lt. G.H. Oldridge, RNR). (3)
20 Dec 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) departs Tobermory for Dundee. She is escorted by the British armed yacht HMS White Bear (Cdr.(Retd.) C.C. Flemming, RN). (3)
22 Dec 1940 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) arrived at Dundee for a refit. During this refit she received a new battery. (3)
13 Feb 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) is put into dock at Dundee. (3)
11 Mar 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H. Tichelman, RNN) is undocked. (3)
31 Mar 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) starts her post-refit trials on the river Tay. (3)
8 Apr 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted exercises off Dundee with HrMs Z 5 (Lt.Cdr. P.A. de Boer, RNN). (5)
11 Apr 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) is docked at Dundee. (3)
15 Apr 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) is undocked. (3)
16 Apr 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducts trials on the river Tay. (3)
19 Apr 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departs Dundee to assist in A/S exercises off Methil. (3)
21 Apr 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises in the Firth of Forth. (3)
24 Apr 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises in the Firth of Forth. Upon completion of these she proceeded to Dundee. (3)
29 Apr 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Dundee for Scapa Flow where she was to participate in A/S exercises. She was escorted by HrMs Z 5 (Lt.Cdr. P.A. de Boer, RNN). (3)
30 Apr 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Scapa Flow. (3)
2 May 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Farndale (Cdr. S.H. Carlill, RN) and HMS St. Albans (Lt.Cdr.(Emgy.) S.G.C. Rawson, RN). (3)
3 May 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow. (3)
4 May 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow. (3)
5 May 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Edinburgh (Capt. C.M. Blackman, DSO, RN). (3)

6 May 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS London (Capt. R.M. Servaes, CBE, RN), HMS Sikh (Cdr. G.H. Stokes, RN) and HMS Walpole (Lt.Cdr. J.H. Eaden, DSC, RN). (3)
7 May 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Inglefield (Capt. P. Todd, DSO, RN) and HMS Antelope (Lt.Cdr. R.B.N. Hicks, DSO, RN). (3)

8 May 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Hermione (Capt. G.N. Oliver, RN), HMS Farndale (Cdr. S.H. Carlill, RN) and HMS Blankney (Lt.Cdr. P.F. Powlett, RN). (3)

9 May 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Escapade (Lt.Cdr. E.N.V. Currey, DSC, RN), HMS Farndale (Cdr. S.H. Carlill, RN), HMS Blankney (Lt.Cdr. P.F. Powlett, RN) and HMS Electra (Cdr. C.W. May, RN). (3)

10 May 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Intrepid (Cdr. R.C. Gordon, DSO, RN) and another destroyer [unable to read the name in the log of HrMs O 14]. (3)
11 May 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departs Scapa Flow for Dundee. She is escorted by the British armed yacht HMS White Bear (Cdr.(Retd.) C.C. Flemming, RN). (3)
12 May 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Dundee. (3)
31 May 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Methil for Scapa Flow. She makes the passage in a convoy. (3)
1 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Scapa Flow for another period of A/S exercises. (3)
2 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Lance (Lt.Cdr. R.W.F. Northcott, RN) and HNoMS Bath (Lt.Cdr. C.F.T. Melsom). (3)
3 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Lightning (Cdr. R.G. Stewart, RN) and HMS Black Swan (Cdr. T.A.C. Pakenham, RN). (3)
4 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Jupiter (Lt.Cdr. N.V.J.P. Thew, RN) and HMAS Nestor (Cdr. A.S. Rosenthal, RAN).
Following these A/S exercises the destroyers conducted other exercises as well. HMAS Nestor returned to Scapa Flow around 2100 B/4 and HMS Jupiter around 0300 B/5. (6)
5 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Stork (Lt. G.T.S. Gray, DSC, RN) and HMS Black Swan (Cdr. T.A.C. Pakenham, RN). (3)
6 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Lightning (Cdr. R.G. Stewart, RN) and HMIS Sutlej (Capt. P.A. Mare, RIN). (3)

7 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Jupiter (Lt.Cdr. N.V.J.P. Thew, RN), HNoMS Bath (Lt.Cdr. C.F.T. Melsom), HMS Black Swan (Cdr. T.A.C. Pakenham, RN) and HMS Stork (Lt. G.T.S. Gray, DSC, RN). (3)

9 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow, first with HMS Nigeria (Capt. J.G.L. Dundas, RN, flying the flag of Rear-Admiral H.M. Burrough, CB, RN) and then with HNoMS Bath (Lt.Cdr. C.F.T. Melsom) and HMAS Nestor (Cdr. A.S. Rosenthal, RAN). (3)

10 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Lance (Lt.Cdr. R.W.F. Northcott, RN), HMS Hambledon (Lt.Cdr. J.R. Barnes, RN) and HMS Black Swan (Cdr. T.A.C. Pakenham, RN). (3)
11 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Stork (Lt. G.T.S. Gray, DSC, RN). (3)

14 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Impulsive (Lt.Cdr. W.S. Thomas, DSC, RN), HNoMS Bath (Lt.Cdr. C.F.T. Melsom), HMS Lance (Lt.Cdr. R.W.F. Northcott, RN) and HMS Hambledon (Lt.Cdr. J.R. Barnes, RN). (3)

15 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Lance (Lt.Cdr. R.W.F. Northcott, RN), HMS Hambledon (Lt.Cdr. J.R. Barnes, RN), HMS Lightning (Cdr. R.G. Stewart, RN) and HMIS Jumna (Cdr. W.R. Shewring, RIN). (3)

16 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with ORP Krakowiak (Cdr. T. Gorazdowski, ORP), HMS Jupiter (Lt.Cdr. N.V.J.P. Thew, RN), HMS Bedouin (Cdr. J.A. McCoy, DSO, RN) and HMS Punjabi (Cdr. S.A. Buss, MVO, RN). (3)
17 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Charlestown (Lt.Cdr. T. Johnston, RN) and HNoMS Bath (Lt.Cdr. C.F.T. Melsom). (3)

19 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departs Scapa Flow for Dundee where she arrived later on the same day. she made the trip with the M/S trawler HMS Ophelia (T/Lt. S. Bennett, RNVR) as escort. (3)
22 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) shifts from Dundee to Rosyth. (3)
23 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) shifts from Rosyth to Grangemouth for a refit and docking. (3)
25 Jun 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) is docked at Grangemouth. (3)
9 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) is undocked. She will continue her refit at Grangemouth. (3)
15 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) shifts from Grangemouth to Dundee. (3)
17 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted exercises on the river Tay. Upon completion she joins a convoy to Scapa Flow. (3)
18 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Scapa Flow for another period of A/S exercises. (3)
19 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Punjabi (Cdr. S.A. Buss, MVO, RN) and HMS Anthony (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Hodges, RN). (3)

21 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Anthony (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Hodges, RN), HMS Intrepid (Cdr. R.C. Gordon, DSO, RN), HMS Achates (Lt.Cdr. the Viscount Jocelyn, RN) and HMS Escapade (Lt.Cdr. E.N.V. Currey, DSC, RN). (3)
22 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Oribi (Lt.Cdr. J.E.H. McBeath, DSO, RN) and HMS Escapade (Lt.Cdr. E.N.V. Currey, DSC, RN). (3)

23 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Antelope (Lt.Cdr. R.B.N. Hicks, DSO, RN), ORP Kujawiak (Lt.Cdr. L. Lichodziejewski, ORP), HMS Davyk (Skr. C.A. Grimmer, RNR) and (the drifter) HMS Craigroy. (3)
24 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HrMs Isaac Sweers (Cdr. J. Houtsmuller, RNN) and HMS Oribi (Lt.Cdr. J.E.H. McBeath, DSO, RN). (3)

25 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Punjabi (Cdr. S.A. Buss, MVO, RN), HMS Charlestown (Lt.Cdr. T. Johnston, RN), HMS Castleton (Cdr. (Retd.) F.H.E. Skyrme, RN) and HMS Croome (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Hayes, RN). (3)
26 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Heythrop (Lt.Cdr R.S. Stafford, RN) and HMS Oribi (Lt.Cdr. J.E.H. McBeath, DSO, RN). (3)
29 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with RAF aircraft and HMS Palomares (A/Capt.(Retd.) J.H. Jauncey, RN). (3)
30 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Croome (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Hayes, RN) and HrMs Isaac Sweers (Cdr. J. Houtsmuller, RNN). (3)

31 Jul 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS London (Capt. R.M. Servaes, CBE, RN), HrMs Isaac Sweers (Cdr. J. Houtsmuller, RNN) and HMS Heythrop (Lt.Cdr R.S. Stafford, RN).
On completion of the exercises HMS London and HrMs Isaac Sweers both set course to proceed to Greenock, but apparently not in company with each other. (7)
1 Aug 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Heythrop (Lt.Cdr R.S. Stafford, RN). (3)
2 Aug 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Oribi (Lt.Cdr. J.E.H. McBeath, DSO, RN) and HMS Charlestown (Lt.Cdr. T. Johnston, RN). (3)
4 Aug 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Antelope (Lt.Cdr. R.B.N. Hicks, DSO, RN) and HMS Palomares (A/Capt.(Retd.) J.H. Jauncey, RN). (3)
5 Aug 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with RAF aircraft and HMS Croome (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Hayes, RN). (3)

9 Aug 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Scapa Flow for Lerwick where she arrived later the same day. She was escorted by the British ASW trawler HMS Lord Lloyd (T/Lt. M.W. Bond, RNVR). (3)
13 Aug 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Lerwick for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off Bergen.
For the daily positions of HrMs O 14 during this patrol see the map below.
26 Aug 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) ended her 1st war patrol at Lerwick. (8)
28 Aug 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Lerwick for Dundee. She was escorted by the British armed yacht HMS Cutty Sark (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN). (9)
29 Aug 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Dundee. (9)
5 Sep 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Dundee for her 2nd war Patrol, she was ordered to patrol off Utsira (between Bergen and Stavanger).
O 14 was escorted until 2215/06 by the British armed yacht HMS Cutty Sark (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN).
12 Sep 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) ended her 2nd war patrol at Dundee. (8)
14 Oct 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted torpedo firing exercises on the river Tay. (9)
17 Oct 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Dundee for Lerwick. She was escorted by the British ASW trawler HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR). (8)
18 Oct 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Lerwick.
19 Oct 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Lerwick for her 3rd war Patrol. She was ordered to patrol off Froya (between Bergen and Alesund).
27 Oct 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) ended her 3rd war patrol at Lerwick. (8)
28 Oct 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Lerwick for Scapa Flow. She was escorted by the British ASW trawler HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR). (9)
29 Oct 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Scapa Flow. At Scapa Flow she will participate in A/S exercises. (9)
30 Oct 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Lowestoft (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) F.B. Proudfoot, RN) and HMS Chiddingfold (Lt. L.W.L. Argles, RN). (9)

31 Oct 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Lowestoft (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) F.B. Proudfoot, RN), HMS Chiddingfold (Lt. L.W.L. Argles, RN), HMS Montrose (Lt.Cdr. W.J. Phipps, OBE, RN) and HMS Antelope (Lt.Cdr. E.N. Sinclair, RN). (9)

1 Nov 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Chiddingfold (Lt. L.W.L. Argles, RN), HMS Hurworth (Lt.Cdr. J.T.B. Birch, RN), HMS Lowestoft (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) F.B. Proudfoot, RN) and HMS Southwold (Cdr. C.T. Jellicoe, DSC, RN). (9)
3 Nov 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Onslow (Capt. H.T. Armstrong, DSC and Bar, RN) and HMS Hurworth (Lt.Cdr. J.T.B. Birch, RN). (9)

4 Nov 1941 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMAS Norman (Cdr. H.M. Burrell, RAN), HMS Montrose (Lt.Cdr. W.J. Phipps, OBE, RN) and HMS Hurworth (Lt.Cdr. J.T.B. Birch, RN). (9)

3 Jan 1942 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Scapa Flow for her 4th war patrol. She was to proceed to a position of Hovden, Norway where she was to act as a beacon for the British destroyers HMS Inglefield (Capt. P. Todd, DSO, RN) and HMS Intrepid (Lt.Cdr. J.H. Lewes, DSC, RN), that were to conduct a raid on German shipping in that area [Operation Kitbag].
O 14 was escorted to the North-West of the Shetlands by the British ASW trawler HMS Stella Pegasi (T/Lt. F.S. Howell, RNR). (8)
For the daily positions of HrMs O 14 during her 4th war patrol see the map below.
The object of this operation was an anti-shipping sweep in the Helle Fjord area.

The destroyers HMS Inglefield (Capt. P. Todd, DSO, RN) and HMS Intrepid (Lt.Cdr. J.H. Lewes, DSC, RN) departed Scapa Flow around 0300 A/6. In the afternoon, after having completed exercises off Scapa Flow, the light cruiser HMS Sheffield (Capt. A.W. Clarke, RN) proceeded to a position so as to provide cover for the destroyers.
The Dutch submarine HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) was deployed as a navigational beacon to guide the destroyers in.

The destroyers first proceeded to the anchorage near Botnane but found it empty. They then proceeded to bombard the anchorage quays at Floro. A merchant vessel of about 2000 tons was claimed to have been sunk and damage was done to two trawlers, a cold storage plant and a canning factory. The destroyers retired from the area around midnight.
HMS Sheffield returned to Scapa Flow early in the afternoon of the 7th.
The destroyers returned to Scapa Flow around 1530 A/7.
In fact the German transport Hedwigshütte (2240 GRT, built 1923) had been damaged while the patrol vessel V 5104 / Orkan had been grounded. (10)

8 Apr 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Stevenstone (Lt. P.B.N. Lewis, DSC, RN). (13)

9 Apr 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Tuscan (Lt.Cdr. G.I.M. Balfour, RN), HMS Lauderdale (Lt. G.D. Pound, DSC, RN), HMS Stevenstone (Lt. P.B.N. Lewis, DSC, RN) and HMS Anthony (Lt.Cdr. J.H. Wallace, DSC, RN). (13)

10 Apr 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Anthony (Lt.Cdr. J.H. Wallace, DSC, RN), HMS Lauderdale (Lt. G.D. Pound, DSC, RN) and HMS Stevenstone (Lt. P.B.N. Lewis, DSC, RN). (13)

12 Apr 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Stevenstone (Lt. P.B.N. Lewis, DSC, RN), HMS Milne (Capt. I.M.R. Campbell, DSO, RN), HMS Troubridge (Capt. C.L. Firth, MVO, RN) and HMS Tuscan (Lt.Cdr. G.I.M. Balfour, RN). (13)

13 Apr 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Brecon (Lt.Cdr. T.D. Herrick, DSC and Bar, RN), HMS Seagull (Lt.Cdr. C.H. Pollock, RN), HMS Active (Lt.Cdr. P.G. Merriman, RN) and HMS Stevenstone (Lt. P.B.N. Lewis, DSC, RN). (13)

14 Apr 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Active (Lt.Cdr. P.G. Merriman, RN), HMS Echo (Lt. R.H.C. Wyld, RN) and later with local defence craft. (13)
15 Apr 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Tuscan (Lt.Cdr. G.I.M. Balfour, RN) and HMS Active (Lt.Cdr. P.G. Merriman, RN). (13)

16 Apr 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMCS Iroquois (Cdr. W.B.L. Holms, RCN), HMS Mahratta (Lt.Cdr. E.A.F. Drought, DSC, RN), HMS Harrier (Cdr. A.D.H. Jay, DSO, DSC, RN) and HMS Seagull (Lt.Cdr. C.H. Pollock, RN). (13)
17 Apr 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Harrier (Cdr. A.D.H. Jay, DSO, DSC, RN) and HMS Active (Lt.Cdr. P.G. Merriman, RN). (13)

19 Apr 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Milne (Capt. I.M.R. Campbell, DSO, RN) and HMS Faulknor (Capt. A.K. Scott-Moncrieff, RN). (13)

20 Apr 1943 In the morning, HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMCS Iroquois (Cdr. W.B.L. Holms, RCN), HMS Active (Lt.Cdr. P.G. Merriman, RN) and HMS Tuscan (Lt.Cdr. G.I.M. Balfour, RN).
In the afternoon A/S exercises were carried out with HMS Tumult (Lt.Cdr. N. Lanyon, RN). (17)

22 Apr 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) departed Scapa Flow for Lerwick where she arrived later the same day. She was escorted by the British M/S trawler HMS Hamlet (T/Lt. H.H. Bolton, RNVR). (13)
25 Apr 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) departed Lerwick for her 8th war patrol. She was ordered to perform an anti-uboat patrol in the Norwegian Sea.
6 May 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) ended her 8th war patrol at Lerwick. She was escorted by the British ASW trawler HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR). (8)

8 May 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) arrived at Dundee. During her last patrol O 14 again developed engine trouble. It was decided not to repair the submarine. She was to be decommissioned and used to provide spare parts for her sister ship O 15.
Before O 14 was decommissioned she was used for A/S training off the Firth of Forth. Only the Starboard diesel engine was operational. (18)
26 May 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises in the Firth of Forth with HMS Wolfhound(Lt. J.H.A. Benians, DSC, RN) and HMS Wallace (Lt. D. Carson, RN). (13)
27 May 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises in the Firth of Forth with HMS Winchester (Lt. J.R. Gower, RN) and local defence craft. (13)
28 May 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises in the Firth of Forth with HMS Vanity (Lt.Cdr. W.B.R. Morrison, RN) and local defence craft. (13)

1 Jun 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises in the Firth of Forth with HMS Leeds (Lt. B.H. Brown, RN), HMS Lady Beryl and HMS Stella Conopus (T/Skr. L. Ford, RNR). HMS Vivien (Lt. L.E. Blackmore, RN). (13)

2 Jun 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises in the Firth of Forth with HMS Brimness (Skr. A.E. Larner, RNR), HMS Kingston Onyx, HMS Polka (T/Lt. I.A. Thorpe, MBE, RNVR) and HMS Berkshire (Skr. G. Bruce, RNR). (13)

3 Jun 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises in the Firth of Forth with Verdun (Lt.Cdr. D. Parsons, RD, RNR), HMS Lady Beryl, HMS Polka (T/Lt. I.A. Thorpe, MBE, RNVR) and HMS Berkshire (Skr. G. Bruce, RNR). (13)

4 Jun 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises in the Firth of Forth with HMS Westminster (Lt.Cdr. H.G. Bowerman, DSC, RN), HMS Brimness (Skr. A.E. Larner, RNR), HMS Vega (Lt. C.G. Forsberg, RN) and HMS Wallace (Lt. D. Carson, RN). (13)

6 Jun 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises in the Firth of Forth with HMS Lady Beryl, HMS Brimness (Skr. A.E. Larner, RNR) and HMS Winchester (Lt. J.R. Gower, RN). (13)
8 Jun 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) participated in A/S exercises in the Firth of Forth. (13)
9 Jun 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) conducted A/S exercises in the Firth of Forth with HMS Vivien (Lt. L.E. Blackmore, RN). (13)
